title: Linux Terminal Basics
description: Learn essential commands for directory navigation, file listing, folder creation, reading logs, and cleanup tasks.

tasks.prompt: Print the absolute path of the current working directory.
tasks.explanation: The 'pwd' (Print Working Directory) command returns the exact absolute path of the directory you are currently in.

tasks.prompt: List all files in the current directory, including hidden files (files starting with a dot) in a long listing format.
tasks.hint: Use 'ls' with flags to show all (-a) and use long format (-l)
tasks.explanation: The 'ls' command listing options can be combined. '-la' or '-al' shows permissions, ownership, sizes, and dot-hidden config files.

tasks.prompt: Create a new subdirectory named 'backups' in the current folder.
tasks.hint: Use the make directory command: 'mkdir backups'
tasks.explanation: The 'mkdir' (Make Directory) command creates one or more new directories.

tasks.prompt: Change the active working directory to '/var/log'.
tasks.explanation: The 'cd' command switches your session shell context to a different folder. '/var/log' is a common system log path on Linux.

tasks.prompt: Display the text contents of the file named 'syslog' onto the standard output screen.
tasks.explanation: The 'cat' (concatenate) command outputs a file's entire content to the terminal window. 'less' is also acceptable for paginated viewing.

tasks.prompt: Remove the directory named 'temp' and all of its contents (recursively) without asking for confirmation (forcefully).
tasks.hint: Use remove command with recursive (-r) and force (-f) flags: 'rm -rf temp'
tasks.explanation: The 'rm -rf' command removes directories and files recursively (-r) and forcefully (-f) without prompts. Use with caution!
